markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has locked in a November 19, 2026 console launch for Grand Theft Auto VI after two official delays from the original 2025 window, with Take-Two Interactive tying roughly $8 billion in fiscal 2027 net bookings guidance directly to the title’s on-time arrival. Recent earnings commentary from CEO Strauss Zelnick emphasized the date as firm, underscoring how further slips would disrupt both revenue targets and investor expectations. Marketing momentum is expected to accelerate this summer ahead of the holiday-season window, while the open-world sequel’s massive cultural footprint continues to shape broader entertainment speculation. Traders monitoring the multi-outcome market should watch for any late-cycle signals from Rockstar or Take-Two that could shift the effective deadline.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent developments, including the May 15, 2026 mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York retrial on the Jessica Mann charge after a hung jury, have reinforced trader consensus around limited additional prison time. Following the 2025 mixed verdict—guilty on one criminal sexual act count tied to Miriam Haley but deadlocked elsewhere—Weinstein faces sentencing only on that conviction while already serving a consecutive 16-year California sentence from his 2022 rape conviction. Ongoing appeals on both the overturned 2020 New York conviction and the California case create significant uncertainty, with historical patterns of prolonged legal battles and potential plea considerations supporting the market’s heavy weighting toward no further incarceration beyond current terms. Upcoming hearings, such as the June 2026 status conference, remain key swing factors.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The four teams remaining in the 2026 NHL playoffs—Vegas Golden Knights, Carolina Hurricanes, Montreal Canadiens, and Colorado Avalanche—drive the tight Stanley Cup futures market, with each holding a plausible path through the conference finals and beyond. Vegas gained ground after taking Game 1 from the Avalanche in the West, while the Hurricanes enter their Eastern matchup against the Canadiens as series favorites following strong regular-season and playoff form. Montreal’s unexpected run to the conference finals and Colorado’s prior dominance keep both sides competitive. Trader consensus reflects balanced series probabilities and the inherent unpredictability of playoff hockey, where home-ice advantage, goaltending, and momentum shifts can quickly alter outcomes.

Vegas gained ground after taking Game 1 from the Avalanche in the West, while the Hurricanes enter their Eastern matchup against the Canadiens as series favorites following strong regular-season and playoff form. Montreal’s unexpected run to the conference finals and Colorado’s prior dominance keep both sides competitive. Trader consensus reflects balanced series probabilities and the inherent unpredictability of playoff hockey, where home-ice advantage, goaltending, and momentum shifts can quickly alter outcomes.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France and Spain lead the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market at nearly identical implied probabilities because both enter with unmatched squad depth, proven tournament pedigree, and recent dominance in UEFA competitions. Spain, the Euro 2024 champions under Luis de la Fuente, maintained strong qualifying form and boast elite young attackers alongside experienced midfield control. France, 2018 winners and 2022 finalists under Didier Deschamps, similarly excelled in qualification while relying on versatile forwards and defensive organization. England follows closely on the back of consecutive European Championship final appearances and midfield strength. The tight clustering reflects broad trader consensus on the parity among these European sides heading into an expanded 48-team field, where minor injury updates or late form shifts could still reshape outcomes.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's firm grip on the Chinese Communist Party, secured through the 2022 abolition of term limits and subsequent personnel loyalist placements, continues to underpin the 93.5% trader consensus against his removal before 2027. Recent anti-corruption drives and military leadership adjustments have further centralized authority without evidence of coordinated elite opposition or factional splits capable of forcing an early exit. With the 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027, analysts see continuity in his roles as General Secretary and Central Military Commission chairman rather than transition pressures. Ongoing high-level diplomacy and policy directives on economic planning through 2030 reinforce institutional stability, while health or succession rumors lack substantiation in official actions or verifiable reporting. This environment leaves few plausible pathways for ouster within the market's resolution window ending December 2026.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading trader consensus at 24 percent in this wide-open 2028 Democratic field because his California governorship provides executive experience and term limits free him for national positioning after the November midterms.
